middleware.description: Applied to provider webhook routes that carry chargeback-typed events. Delegates to payment::PaymentWebhookVerifier for signature verification (this module does NOT own signature-verification code — the payment module does). On success: dispatches ChargebackWebhookReceived event which the HandleChargebackWebhook listener consumes. On failure: returns 403 `CHARGEBACK_WEBHOOK_INVALID_SIGNATURE` OR 409 `CHARGEBACK_WEBHOOK_STALE_TIMESTAMP`.
auto_applied_to: Provider webhook routes owned by payment module — this middleware is a thin adapter

middleware.description: Thin wrapper over entitlement.enforce that specifically checks the `chargeback_management` entitlement. On refusal returns 402 `CHARGEBACK_MANAGEMENT_NOT_ENTITLED` with an upgrade CTA in the response envelope's .errors[0].meta.upgrade_url. Auto-applied via the `entitlement.enforce:chargeback_management` middleware chain on every /api/v1/chargebacks/* route.

consumed.note: The permission-based authorize middleware wraps the ability checks defined in policies.json + permissions.json.
Chargeback webhook verification delegates to the payment module's verifier. Signature-verification code is centralized there.
Rate-limit bucket for admin chargeback routes. Default: 120 requests/min per authenticated admin. Generous — admin workflows may bulk-process evidence.
Rate-limit bucket for platform-plane cross-tenant support routes. Default: 60 requests/min per platform_admin.
